summary: boardstep downloads and executes remote.exe payload via install lifecycle hooks
details: package.json registers install.js as preinstall, install, and postinstall hooks. install.js (line 10) hardcodes PAYLOAD_URL = 'https://www.pooron.org/ice.exe', downloads the binary to a randomly named file in the TEMP directory, chmods it 755, and spawns it detached with stdio ignored and windowsHide true on win32, darwin, and linux (install.js line 62). Errors are silently swallowed and the script prints 'Optional dependency initialized' as cover. The package additionally declares itself as a dependency ('boardstep': '^1.0.9') and uses a random-looking author handle, consistent with a throwaway dropper masquerading as a kanban board utility. On `npm install boardstep`, every installer machine fetches and runs an unsigned, unpinned executable from a non-publisher domain.

cwes.description: The product contains code that appears to be malicious in nature.
cwes.name: Embedded Malicious Code
